repository.name: move-anything-waveform-editor
repository.description: Waveform Editor tool for Move Everything - trim, gain adjust, normalize, and edit WAV files on the Ableton Move

commit.message:
Refactor DSP to stereo, fix drum preset export, and misc improvements

- Refactor DSP from mono int16 to interleaved stereo int16 (L,R,L,R,...)
- Mono WAV files duplicated to both channels on load
- Support PCM 16-bit, PCM 24-bit, and IEEE float 32-bit input
- WAV output always stereo 16-bit PCM
- Waveform display and zero-crossing use L channel
- Fix DSP export symbol: plugin_init -> move_plugin_init_v2
- Fix build.sh to copy from src/dsp/dsp.so (Docker output path)
- Fix drum preset sampleUri: URL-encode spaces (%20)
- Default auto slice threshold from 5 to 25
- Disable loop playback in slice mode, restore on exit
- Back from record-ready state exits module entirely

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix recording race condition and improve recorded file handling

- Add "stopping" state to poll sampler finalization before loading WAV,
  preventing "No waveform data" error from async stop
- Show "SAVING..." screen while waiting for WAV to finalize
- Track recorded files as unsaved (dirty) with discard-on-exit cleanup
- Add timestamp to edit filenames (sample_edit_0306_1423.wav)
- Clean up recording UI header (remove debug VU display)
- Add dirty param support to DSP plugin

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Remap knobs: E4=vertical scale, E5=gain/threshold, E7/E8=twist actions

- E3: zoom only (removed shift+E3 vertical scale)
- E4: vertical scale for all modes (was shift+E3)
- E5: gain/normalize in trim/loop, threshold in slice auto (was E4)
- E7 touch+twist: toggle loop on/off
- E8 touch+twist: snap to zero crossing
- Twist prompts stay visible while knob is touched
- Add snapToZeroCrossing() helper function
- Recording waveform: scrolling write-head visualization
- Recording stop: poll for sampler finalization before loading
- Shift+pad preview: 5% of selection, clamped 0.25s-5s
- Update help.json for new knob layout

commit.message:
fix: bypass getBundledCliPath by finding copilot CLI via system PATH

The SDK's getBundledCliPath() uses import.meta.resolve("@github/copilot/sdk")
which fails when running from a single-file bundle with no local node_modules.
Find the globally-installed copilot binary via 'which copilot' and pass it as
cliPath to CopilotClient so that code path is never reached.

commit.message:
Fix page-level cursor color ignored on frontend

get_cursor_context_document() returned template_document (header/footer)
instead of the actual page document, so page-level settings like Cursor
Color were never read. Since templates no longer have cursor controls,
removed template_document priority — always use page document.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix white screen: pass secrets as env vars to expo export

EXPO_PUBLIC_* variables get baked into the JS bundle at build time.
The .env file is gitignored, so CI built with undefined Supabase/Anthropic
credentials causing a silent crash. Now pulls from GitHub Actions secrets.

To make this work, add these 3 secrets in GitHub →
Settings → Secrets and variables → Actions:
  EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL
  EXPO_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY
  EXPO_PUBLIC_ANTHROPIC_API_KEY

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore(cli): rename npm package to anytext-cli

The name "anytext" conflicts with existing npm package "any-text".
The binary command remains "anytext".

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add GitHub Actions workflow for daily collection and Pages deployment

Runs daily at 06:00 UTC to collect energy data and deploy the
dashboard to GitHub Pages. Database persisted on a data-store branch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix T2V upscale artifacts: use manual sigmas from official LTX 2.3 workflow

T2V upscale was producing blob artifacts because the re-diffusion sigma
schedule was computed from the upscaled latent token count (~20k tokens,
shift ~7.7), injecting far too much noise. The official workflow uses
hand-tuned manual sigmas [0.909375, 0.725, 0.421875, 0.0] with default
shift (tokens=4096, shift ~2.05). I2V upscale path is unchanged.

commit.message:
Fix agent self-update ETXTBSY and services collector on DietPi

Self-update: stage download in /var/lib/riot/ then use sudo install
to atomically replace the running binary via new inode. Fixes text
file busy error. Added sudoers rule for the exact install command.

Services collector: systemctl list-units returns exit code 1 when
degraded units exist but output is still valid. Use CombinedOutput
and only fail when output is empty.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: mcalcmove m_moving parameter matching C mon.c

C's mcalcmove(mon, m_moving) only does random rounding (consuming
rn2) when m_moving=true. worm.c calls with m_moving=false for
speed-only calculation. JS was always rounding, consuming an extra
rn2 per worm per turn.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add geo-based incident filtering with HubSpot-ClickUp integration

Features:
- Geo-proximity filtering (10km radius) using Vercel geo headers
- HubSpot → ClickUp clubs sync architecture
- Relationship-based venue lookup
- Distance calculation with Haversine formula
- Automatic filtering based on user location
- Fallback to show all incidents if no geo data

New Components:
- src/lib/geo.ts: Geolocation utilities and distance calculation
- SETUP-GUIDE.md: Complete setup instructions

Updated:
- incidents API: Relationship lookup + geo-filtering
- Types: GolfClub, Coordinates interfaces
- Environment: CLICKUP_CLUBS_LIST_ID added

ClickUp Structure:
- List: "GolfNext Clubs" (synced from HubSpot)
  Fields: HubSpot_ID, Latitude, Longitude, Country, Country_Code, City
- List: "Driftsforstyrrelser"
  Venue: Relationship field → links to clubs

How it works:
1. HubSpot syncs clubs to ClickUp (native integration)
2. Incidents link to clubs via relationship field
3. Support page fetches incidents + club coordinates
4. Filters based on Vercel geo data (user location)
5. Shows only incidents for clubs within 10km

Requires: Vercel Pro (for geo headers), ClickUp clubs list ID

commit.message:
fix: Add channel lead auto-respawn and correct session role labels [Midtown !2106] (#1840)

* fix: Add channel lead auto-respawn and correct session role labels

Two issues caused lead sessions to not recover properly after auth errors:

1. Channel leads (e.g., "ops") had no auto-respawn mechanism. When shut down
   by check_and_handle_auth_errors during an auth storm, they stayed dead
   permanently. Added ensure_channel_leads_alive() — the channel lead
   equivalent of ensure_lead_alive() — with the same cooldown-based respawn.

2. All session exit messages were labeled "Coworker" regardless of role,
   making it hard to diagnose lead vs channel lead vs coworker exits.
   Updated exit messages and auth error messages to use proper role labels
   ("Lead", "Channel lead", "Coworker").

Also updated auth error channel messages from "coworkers" to "sessions"
for role-neutral terminology.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: Skip channel lead respawn when session is attached interactively

ensure_channel_leads_alive() only checked active_coworkers before
respawning, but attached sessions are deregistered from active_coworkers
and tracked in attached_coworkers. After the 5-minute cooldown, this
would spawn a duplicate headless session competing with the interactive
attach. Add the same attached_coworkers guard that ensure_lead_alive()
already has.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: Make ensure_channel_leads_alive a pure function with Effect pattern

Address review feedback on PR #1840:

1. Refactor ensure_channel_leads_alive() from async (with inline I/O via
   load_channel_lead_context) to a pure synchronous function that returns
   Effect::RespawnChannelLead. The effect executor in effects.rs handles
   context loading and session spawning, consistent with the documented
   pure-function pattern for evaluate_tick().

2. Update docs/architecture.md: document ensure_channel_leads_alive() in
   the Health section and add auto-respawn lifecycle documentation to the
   Channel Leads section.

3. Add direct unit tests that call the now-pure function and assert on
   returned effects (respawn, no-op when registered, cooldown, attached).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

commit.message:
fix: sidebar overlay breakpoint for tablet support (#300)

* fix: raise sidebar overlay breakpoint to 1024px for tablet support

Tablets (iPad at 810px) were getting the desktop inline sidebar instead
of the mobile overlay. This caused the collapsed sidebar to eat 56px of
screen space and prevented the overlay open/close UX on touch devices.

- Raise mobile/tablet breakpoint from 768px to 1024px for sidebar CSS
- Match JS isMobile() check to new breakpoint
- Show new-session button in shortcut bar on tablets too
- Add tablet project (iPad gen 7) to Playwright config
- Rewrite sidebar e2e tests with cross-viewport helpers
- Add responsive test coverage for overlay behavior, backdrop close,
  session switching, and space consumption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address review findings — rename isMobile to isOverlayViewport

- Rename isMobile() → isOverlayViewport() and setMobileSidebar() →
  setOverlaySidebar() to match actual semantics (covers tablets too)
- Add breakpoint comment linking CSS and JS values
- Skip "sidebar stays closed" test on overlay viewports where it tests
  the wrong behavior

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Optimize dataset precision: Convert float64 to float32 and archive originals

Added data optimization pipeline:
- convert_to_float32.py: Script to convert NPZ files from float64 to float32
- Created data/raw_float64_original/ directory to preserve original files
  * Backed up all 13 original float64 NPZ files (115 MB)
  * No data loss: Files preserved for validation or reference
  * Safe to delete after verification if needed

Conversion results:
- Original dataset (float64):     120.5 MB
- Optimized dataset (float32):    75.2 MB
- Space saved:                    45.3 MB
- Reduction rate:                 37.6%

Benefits:
- float32 is the default precision for TensorFlow/Keras neural networks
- Maintains full precision for training purposes
- Compatible with GPU acceleration
- Industry-standard for deep learning

Data structure:
- data/raw/                       : float32 optimized files (72 MB local)
- data/raw_float64_original/      : float64 backup files (115 MB local)
- Both directories are locally stored, not committed to git (ignored by .gitignore)

How to use:
1. Keep data/raw/ for training (float32)
2. Reference data/raw_float64_original/ if you need original precision
3. Delete data/raw_float64_original/ if you confirm float32 is sufficient

Load example in src/load_example_npz.py

commit.message:
feat: CI resilience hardening — filesystem-derived routes, preflight, coupling manifest

Replace hardcoded 18-route array in sync-a11y-routes.mjs with recursive
filesystem walker of src/pages/**/*.astro. Eliminates drift-magnet failure
class — new pages are automatically discovered, removed pages automatically
disappear. Remove vestigial project-index.ts regex extraction that produced
21 duplicate routes after walker introduction.

Add npm run preflight (lint + typecheck + build + validate + sync + test)
to catch ~80% of CI failures locally before pushing.

Add .quality/GOVERNANCE-COUPLING.md documenting all known coupling points
with their enforcement mechanisms.

commit.message:
Add Hue K-Means palette detection method

Runs k-means in 1D circular hue space (ignoring luminance and
saturation). Each centroid is updated via circular mean (atan2 of
sin/cos sums). Near-grey pixels are excluded. The final palette color
per cluster is the mean RGB of all member pixels at that hue centroid.
